seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using CrewAI face heightened risks when multi-agent systems process sensitive patient data, where agent delegation loops or hallucinated outputs could expose PHI or lead to misdiagnoses. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent patient harm, regulatory fines up to $1.5M per HIPAA violation, and malpractice liability. Vex provides auditable oversight at every task step, ensuring HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, HITECH, and SOC 2 compliance in agent orchestration.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; requires de-identification before LLM processing and prevents data from leaving secure environments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex intercepts agent tasks to enforce PHI redaction via callbacks, logs all data flows for audit trails, and blocks non-compliant outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, auditable, and tamper-proof for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides immutable audit logs of every agent decision, task delegation, and output validation with cryptographic signatures.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notifications and stricter data security for healthcare AI systems handling PHI.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors for breach risks like agent loops or task drift, alerting on anomalies and ensuring data stays within compliant boundaries.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls over security, availability, and confidentiality in AI platforms processing healthcare data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex's task-level callbacks validate agent outputs against SOC 2 controls, preventing inconsistent quality and ensuring high availability.

industryRisks.name: Agent delegation loops
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly pass tasks between crew members, delaying critical diagnoses and risking PHI exposure through repeated processing.
industryRisks.example: Triage agent loops with diagnosis agent on ambiguous symptoms, causing 2-hour delay in stroke patient care.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents fabricate lab results or medical histories, propagating errors that lead to incorrect treatment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Pharmacist agent hallucinates drug interaction data, recommending contraindicated medication.

industryRisks.name: Task drift
industryRisks.description: Agents reinterpret objectives, deviating from clinical protocols and generating non-compliant outputs.
industryRisks.example: Research agent drifts from de-identification task and processes raw PHI through LLM.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent output quality
industryRisks.description: Variable agent performance across roles leads to unreliable clinical decision support.
industryRisks.example: Junior vs senior clinician agents produce conflicting treatment plans for same patient.

implementation.title: PHI De-identification Callback
implementation.description: Add Vex callback to every CrewAI task to automatically redact PHI before LLM processing, ensuring HIPAA compliance.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

def phi_redact_callback(task, context):
    # Vex automatically detects and redacts PHI
    result = vex.validate_and_redact(context['input'], rules=['hipaa-phi'])
    if result['compliance_status'] == 'FAIL':
        raise ValueError('PHI detected - task blocked')
    context['redacted_input'] = result['sanitized_data']
    return context

# Apply to triage agent task
triage_task = Task(
    description='Triage patient symptoms',
    agent=triage_agent,
    context_callback=phi_redact_callback
)

implementation.title: Loop Detection Middleware
implementation.description: Prevent delegation loops by tracking agent interactions and blocking circular task flows.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Crew
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

class LoopGuard:
    def __init__(self):
        self.task_history = []
    
    def check_loop(self, task_id, agent_id):
        history = vex.get_session_history(task_id)
        if len(history) > 5 and agent_id in [h['agent'] for h in history[-3:]]:
            raise Exception('Delegation loop detected - max iterations exceeded')
        return True

loop_guard = LoopGuard()

crew = Crew(
    agents=[triage_agent, diagnosis_agent],
    tasks=[triage_task, diagnosis_task],
    verbose=True,
    task_callback=lambda task: loop_guard.check_loop(task.id, task.agent.id)
)

implementation.title: Audit Trail for FDA 21 CFR Part 11
implementation.description: Log every agent decision with cryptographic signatures for regulatory audit compliance.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Task
from vex_sdk import VexClient, AuditLog

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

def auditable_task_output(task, output):
    # Create tamper-proof audit record
    audit = AuditLog(
        task_id=task.id,
        agent_id=task.agent.id,
        input_data=task.description,
        output_data=output,
        timestamp='now',
        signature=vex.sign_data(output)
    )
    vex.log_audit(audit)
    
    # Validate against clinical guidelines
    compliance = vex.validate_medical_output(output, guidelines='chemo_protocol')
    if not compliance['valid']:
        vex.alert('Non-compliant medical recommendation')
    
    return output

chemo_task = Task(
    description='Recommend chemotherapy regimen',
    agent=oncology_agent,
    output_callback=auditable_task_output
)

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Patel's oncology practice deployed CrewAI to streamline chemotherapy regimen planning. The crew consisted of a triage agent screening patient histories, a pharmacology agent checking drug interactions, and an oncology specialist agent generating treatment recommendations. Patient data including recent labs, genomic profiles, and medication histories flowed through the multi-agent system daily.

Without compliance guardrails, the system quickly developed issues: the triage agent began looping with the pharmacology agent over ambiguous allergy data, hallucinating penicillin sensitivity that blocked appropriate beta-lactam antibiotics. Task drift occurred when the oncology agent reinterpreted 'aggressive treatment' as experimental therapies outside FDA-approved protocols. Raw PHI including SSNs and MRNs passed unredacted through LLMs, violating HIPAA. Inconsistent outputs between agents created conflicting recommendations, confusing clinicians and delaying care.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails transformed the deployment. PHI redaction callbacks blocked 97% of unredacted data flows, ensuring HIPAA compliance. Loop detection terminated circular delegations after 3 iterations, reducing processing time from 45 minutes to 4 minutes. Medical knowledge validation rejected 12 hallucinated drug interactions and enforced NCCN guidelines across all outputs. Immutable audit logs satisfied FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements during inspection, with every decision cryptographically signed. The practice processed 250+ patients monthly with zero compliance violations and 30% faster clinical workflows.
